title: Astronauts See the Moon's Hidden Side for the First Time
summary: A group of astronauts on a mission called Artemis II have seen the far side of the Moon for the first time. The crew includes four people: Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen. They are traveling in a spacecraft named Orion and are on their third day of the trip. They took a photo of a special area on the Moon called the Orientale basin, which no human had ever seen completely before. The spacecraft is very far from Earth, over 180,000 miles away. The astronauts described the view as 'absolutely spectacular' and different from the Moon we usually see. This mission is exciting because it helps us learn more about space and prepares for future trips to the Moon.

background_read: The Moon is Earth's only natural satellite, and we usually see the same side from Earth. The far side is hidden and was first photographed by a spacecraft in 1959. NASA's Artemis program aims to send astronauts back to the Moon, with Artemis II being a test flight around it without landing. Astronauts train for years to go on such missions, and this trip helps scientists understand space travel better for future explorations.
Article_Structure: WHO: Four astronauts: Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen
WHAT: They saw the far side of the Moon for the first time and took a photo of the Orientale basin
WHERE: In the Orion spacecraft traveling around the Moon
WHY: To explore space and prepare for future Moon missions

perspectives.description: This mission shows human curiosity and the desire to discover new things in space.
